source_completeness.note: All 42 MET dimensions have at least one confirmed source meeting the Grade standard. 12 entries are classified minor_gaps: 8 batch-1 entries with minor URL or verification gaps (non-blocking), plus the 4 batch-2 MET dimensions pending an editorial completeness pass.

D1.name: AI Chip Design
D1.description: Designs and commercially sells training-class AI accelerators under domestic IP control.

D2.name: Advanced Fabrication
D2.description: Operates production-capable semiconductor fabrication at process nodes of 7nm or below.

D3.name: Critical Equipment
D3.description: Hosts entities that design and manufacture globally essential semiconductor production equipment or sole-sourced subsystems for advanced fabrication.

D4.name: AI Compute
D4.description: Hosts hyperscale cloud providers operating AI training infrastructure sufficient to train current-generation frontier models, with compute physically on the actor's territory.

D5.name: Frontier Models
D5.description: Hosts organisations developing state-of-the-art foundation models competitive with the current global frontier across multiple capability domains.

D6.name: AI Regulation
D6.description: Enforces AI-relevant compliance frameworks with binding obligations for entities outside its own jurisdiction.

D7.name: Sovereign Investment
D7.description: Operates a national programme directing significant public capital toward AI compute, semiconductor fabrication, or AI-specific physical infrastructure at nationally transformative scale.

PAA.name: Principal AI Actor
PAA.description: Ecosystem anchor: scores ≥3 dimensions across ≥2 capability categories, passes the per-PAA severance test, and holds at least one sustainable platform dimension.
PAA.gate_requirements: Gate 1 (≥3 dimensions across ≥2 capability categories) + Gate 2 (per-PAA severance test PASS) + Gate 3 (≥1 sustainable platform dimension)

AIK.name: AI Infrastructure Keystone
AIK.description: Chokepoint leverage without ecosystem provision: scores ≥3 dimensions across ≥2 capability categories, passes the per-PAA severance test, but holds no sustainable platform dimension.
AIK.gate_requirements: Gate 1 (≥3 dimensions across ≥2 capability categories) + Gate 2 (per-PAA severance test PASS) + Gate 3 (no sustainable platform dimension)

ACS.name: Advanced Capability State
ACS.description: Significant sovereign capabilities that cannot be sustained autonomously under withdrawal of a PAA’s ecosystem inputs: scores ≥3 dimensions across ≥2 capability categories but fails the per-PAA severance test.
ACS.gate_requirements: Gate 1 (≥3 dimensions across ≥2 capability categories) + Gate 2 (per-PAA severance test FAIL)

Participant.name: Participant
Participant.description: Operates within ecosystem(s) shaped by PAAs and AIKs. Per-dimension data carries the analytical detail.

actors.name: United States
actors.severance_detail: All 7 dimensions sustainable under severance. Full-stack ecosystem anchor.

primary.qualifying_fact: Data Centre revenue $193.7 billion for FY2026, up 68%. Blackwell Ultra leading architecture across all customer categories.

corroborating.qualifying_fact: Trainium fully subscribed with 1.4 million chips landed across all generations. Combined custom silicon annual revenue run rate over US$10 billion (Trainium, Graviton, Nitro).

D1.qualification_headline: Three independent training-class accelerator programmes — NVIDIA Blackwell Ultra, AMD Instinct, Trainium — under US IP control
D1.qualification: NVIDIA Data Centre revenue reached US$193.7 billion (FY2026, up 68%) with Blackwell Ultra as leading architecture. Amazon deployed 1.4 million Trainium chips; combined custom silicon revenue exceeds US$10 billion annually. AMD ships the Instinct MI series. No other jurisdiction hosts more than one competitive programme.

primary.qualifying_fact: Fab 52 fully operational, manufacturing Intel 18A wafers — most advanced logic wafers produced in the United States at time of assessment.

corroborating.qualifying_fact: TSMC Arizona site in volume production since late 2024.

D2.qualification_headline: Intel Fab 52 manufactures Intel 18A wafers and TSMC Arizona is in volume production, both operating on US territory
D2.qualification: Intel's Fab 52 is fully operational producing Intel 18A wafers — the most advanced logic process manufactured in the United States at time of assessment. TSMC's Arizona facility entered volume production in late 2024. Both sites operate on US soil under territorial jurisdiction regardless of corporate parent.

primary.qualifying_fact: KLA increased metrology and inspection share from 72.6% in 2024 to 73.8% in 2025.

corroborating.qualifying_fact: CEO: KLA is indispensable to advanced process control regardless of fab operator.

D3.qualification_headline: KLA holds 73.8% of the global metrology and inspection market, described as indispensable to advanced process control
D3.qualification: KLA increased its metrology and inspection market share from 72.6% in 2024 to 73.8% in 2025. Applied Materials and Lam Research hold dominant positions in deposition and etch respectively. No advanced fabrication facility at any node operates without US-designed process control equipment.

primary.qualifying_fact: xAI Colossus: 100,000 NVIDIA Hopper GPUs, built in 122 days. Expanded to 555,000 GPUs at 2 GW.

corroborating.qualifying_fact: Anthropic, xAI, Google, OpenAI all occupy the top tier on Arena Elo.

D4.qualification_headline: xAI Colossus comprises 100,000 GPUs expanded to 555,000 at 2 GW; multiple US hyperscalers train frontier models on US territory
D4.qualification: xAI's Colossus cluster operates 100,000 NVIDIA Hopper GPUs, built in 122 days, and expanded to 555,000 GPUs at 2 GW capacity. Anthropic, xAI, Google, and OpenAI all occupy the top tier on Arena Elo rankings. US territory hosts the largest concentration of frontier-class AI training infrastructure globally at time of assessment.

primary.qualifying_fact: Frontier models gained 30 percentage points in a single year on Humanity's Last Exam.

corroborating.qualifying_fact: Multiple US-headquartered models (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, xAI) at global frontier across reasoning, coding, and knowledge benchmarks.

D5.qualification_headline: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, and xAI all occupy the top benchmark tier; no other jurisdiction hosts four frontier labs
D5.qualification: Stanford HAI's 2026 AI Index confirms Anthropic, xAI, Google, and OpenAI in the top Arena Elo tier. Frontier models gained 30 percentage points on Humanity's Last Exam in a single year. Multiple US-headquartered organisations hold frontier positions across reasoning, coding, and knowledge benchmarks.

primary.qualifying_fact: Prosecution for conspiracy to violate export controls by illegally exporting advanced GPUs to the PRC.

corroborating.qualifying_fact: Conspiracy to export at least US$160 million in export-controlled NVIDIA H100 and H200 GPUs. Over US$50 million seized. Guilty pleas obtained.

D6.qualification_headline: Export controls enforced extraterritorially — Operation Gatekeeper prosecuted US$160 million GPU smuggling conspiracy
D6.qualification: The Department of Justice prosecuted conspiracy to export at least US$160 million in controlled GPUs destined for China. Operation Gatekeeper seized over US$50 million in NVIDIA H100 and H200 GPUs and obtained guilty pleas, demonstrating active extraterritorial enforcement of AI-relevant export control instruments.

primary.publisher: US Department of Commerce (CHIPS for America)
primary.qualifying_fact: CHIPS for America awarded over $33 billion of the over $36 billion in proposed incentives funding.

corroborating.qualifying_fact: Intel received $5.695 billion in CHIPS incentives; issued 274.6M shares of common stock and a warrant.

D7.qualification_headline: CHIPS for America awarded over US$33 billion in fabrication incentives; Intel alone received US$5.7 billion
D7.qualification: The CHIPS for America programme awarded over US$33 billion of the US$36 billion in proposed incentives, directed at semiconductor fabrication on US territory. Intel received US$5.695 billion in CHIPS incentives and issued equity instruments. The programme is the largest single sovereign investment in domestic semiconductor manufacturing at time of assessment.

D1.qualification_headline: Huawei's Ascend 910B/910C uses the domestically designed DaVinci architecture with confirmed commercial hyperscale deployment

D2.qualification_headline: SMIC produces 7nm-class wafers using multi-patterning DUV lithography; DUV-only fabrication with no EUV access

D3.constraint_headline: No Chinese tool globally non-substitutable at ≤7nm; all functions replaceable by US/Japan/Netherlands alternatives

D4.qualification_headline: Frontier training on Chinese territory via Ascend clusters and pre-restriction NVIDIA H800; Ascend performance gap persists

D5.qualification_headline: Alibaba and DeepSeek place in the top Arena Elo tier alongside US frontier labs across multiple capability domains

D6.qualification_headline: Article 49 extends jurisdiction to transfers outside China; REE extraterritorial controls exercised then suspended

D7.qualification_headline: Big Fund III raised RMB 344 billion (US$47.5 billion) from 19 state-backed investors for semiconductor development

aik_profile.key_entities: TEL coater/developer (~90–100% EUV), Lasertec actinic mask inspection (100%), SCREEN single-wafer cleans, Shin-Etsu/JSR/TOK/SUMCO materials

D1.constraint_headline: Only domestic training accelerator (PFN MN-Core 2) deployed in low thousands; Japan's own flagship HPC selects NVIDIA for AI workloads

D2.constraint_headline: Zero ≤7nm fabs in volume manufacturing; Rapidus 2nm in pilot only, mass production targeted H2 FY2027

D3.qualification_headline: TEL holds ~100% EUV coater/developer share and Lasertec sole-sources actinic mask inspection; US-dependency exposure documented

D4.qualification_headline: SoftBank cluster delivers 13.7 ExaFLOPS on Japanese territory; domestically scaled but GPU procurement dependent on US ecosystem

D5.constraint_headline: No Japanese-developed model places in the global top tier on any canonical discriminating benchmark

D6.qualification_headline: FEFTA controls 23 types of advanced semiconductor manufacturing equipment; catch-all controls strengthened October 2025

D7.qualification_headline: Japan's FY2026 semiconductor-and-AI budget nearly quadrupled to approximately ¥1.23 trillion; separately, cumulative state R&D backing for Rapidus reached ¥2.35 trillion

aik_profile.chokepoint_nature: Memory packaging, fabrication, and sovereign equipment monopolies
aik_profile.key_entities: HPSP sole-source high-pressure annealing, Hanmi 71.2% TC bonder share, Samsung Foundry 2nm GAA HVM

D1.constraint_headline: All shipping Korean-designed accelerators are inference-only; training compute runs on imported NVIDIA silicon

D2.qualification_headline: Samsung Foundry produces at 3nm and commenced 2nm GAA mass production; fabrication dependent on imported ASML EUV equipment

D3.qualification_headline: HPSP is sole-source for high-pressure annealing and Hanmi holds 71.2% TC bonder share; FDPR and BIS exposure documented

D4.constraint_headline: Largest coherent Korean-owned training cluster is ~4,000 GPUs; major facilities are US-hyperscaler-anchored

D5.constraint_headline: Best Korean models score ~25-30 points below frontier flagships on aggregated capability leaderboards

D6.qualification_headline: AI Basic Act (Law 20676) Article 4(1) applies to acts conducted abroad affecting the Korean domestic market or users

D7.qualification_headline: KRW 10.1 trillion allocated in the 2026 AI budget; AI Growth Fund expanded to over KRW 150 trillion

D1.constraint_headline: No EU entity designs a datacenter training accelerator; closest candidate (SiPearl Rhea1) is an HPC CPU for inference

D2.qualification_headline: Intel Fab 34 in Ireland is the first European facility using EUV in volume production; the facility is US-owned

D3.qualification_headline: ASML shipped 44 EUV systems as sole global supplier; Zeiss holds 100% EUV optics share — Cymer light source under US jurisdiction

D4.constraint_headline: Largest EU-controlled coherent training cluster is ~1,016 H100s; frontier training requires 50,000-200,000+

D5.constraint_headline: Mistral's best model scores 39/100 on composite intelligence index; frontier models score 57-61

D6.qualification_headline: EU AI Act Article 2(1)(c) applies to providers placing AI systems on the EU market regardless of establishment location

D7.qualification_headline: EuroHPC mandate expanded with AI Gigafactories programme; JUPITER operational at exascale, LUMI at pre-exascale

D1.constraint_headline: Taiwan's ASIC design firms execute foreign clients' architectures; core accelerator IP remains abroad

D2.qualification_headline: TSMC operates 3nm volume production at Fab 18 with N2 GAA on roadmap; fabrication dependent on US-origin equipment and EDA tools

D3.qualification_headline: Gudeng Precision holds approximately 80% of the global EUV photomask pod market and is sole supplier to TSMC

D4.constraint_headline: Aggregate national compute (~114 PFLOPS) is roughly two orders of magnitude below frontier-training scale

D5.constraint_headline: All domestic models are foreign-base fine-tunes; none rank on any global benchmark leaderboard

D6.constraint_headline: Export controls bind domestic exporters only; AI Basic Act is soft law with no extraterritorial reach

D7.qualification_headline: NT$30–31.1 billion first-year AI strategy allocation under NSTC; FY2026 budget at legislative risk, compute procures NVIDIA GPUs

aik_profile.chokepoint_nature: Core lithography integration and advanced packaging
aik_profile.key_entities: ASML 100% EUV monopoly, ASM International (ALD/epi), Besi (hybrid bonding)

D1.constraint_headline: Dutch chip designers (NXP, Axelera) produce inference/edge silicon only; no training-class accelerator exists

D2.constraint_headline: No ≤7nm fab on Dutch territory; NXP Nijmegen operates at 200mm (~140nm), slated for closure

D3.qualification_headline: ASML holds 100% EUV monopoly and Besi leads hybrid bonding from Dutch territory; Cymer and multiple US leverage vectors documented

D4.constraint_headline: Nebius Group N.V. Dutch-domiciled; zero qualifying compute on Dutch territory (all operational in Finland)

D5.constraint_headline: GPT-NL targets Dutch-language sovereignty at ~1/100th frontier budget; no Dutch model ranks on global benchmarks

D6.qualification_headline: Strategic Goods Decree expanded semiconductor equipment export controls with national authorisation requirements

D7.qualification_headline: Project Beethoven committed €2.51 billion to the Brainport Eindhoven chip ecosystem; AI Factory received €200 million

D1.constraint_headline: All French AI chip designs use licensed ARM architecture; no sovereign training-class accelerator exists

D2.constraint_headline: Most advanced French volume node is 18nm FD-SOI; no FinFET or GAA fab exists at any node

D3.constraint_headline: Soitec's SOI substrates serve 22-90nm nodes, not ≤7nm FinFET/GAA process flows

D4.constraint_headline: Largest sovereign cluster (Mistral Compute, ~13,800 GB300s) is commissioning, not operational

D5.constraint_headline: Mistral scores ~35% below frontier on aggregate intelligence indices across key benchmarks

D6.qualification_headline: Exercises EU AI Act authority with demonstrated extraterritorial enforcement — CNIL fined Clearview AI €20 million

D7.qualification_headline: France 2030 provides a €54 billion investment envelope; national AI strategy spending totals €2.4 billion across two phases

D1.constraint_headline: No German entity designs or markets a datacenter AI training accelerator; full NVIDIA dependency for all sovereign compute

D2.constraint_headline: No fab on German territory produces at ≤7nm; Intel Magdeburg cancelled, ESMC Dresden targets ≥12nm only

D3.qualification_headline: Zeiss holds 100% EUV optics share and TRUMPF sole-sources EUV drive lasers; leverage operates through ASML, not independently

D4.constraint_headline: Largest German-controlled AI cluster (~10,000 GPUs / 0.5 ExaFLOPS) is ~1/10th frontier training scale

D5.constraint_headline: No German-controlled entity produces a top-tier foundation model; Aleph Alpha absorbed by Canadian Cohere (90/10 split)

D6.qualification_headline: 21st AWV amendment added AI systems and semiconductor technology to the national export control list in July 2024

D7.constraint_headline: JUPITER is genuine (~€250M national share) but not 'at scale'; collapsed Intel subsidy and foreign-controlled ESMC subsidy cannot be credited

D1.constraint_headline: Sole UK AI accelerator designer (Graphcore) acquired by SoftBank July 2024; no IP-transfer restrictions imposed

D2.constraint_headline: No UK fab operates at or below 7nm; domestic capacity limited to mature and compound semiconductor nodes

D3.constraint_headline: UK equipment firms serve advanced fabs but none is sole-sourced for ≤7nm lithographic fabrication

D4.constraint_headline: No UK-HQ provider operates frontier-training-class compute on UK soil at assessment date; Nscale flagship facility scheduled Q4 2026-Q1 2027

D5.constraint_headline: DeepMind IP owned by Alphabet (US); no independent UK lab ranks top-tier across broad reasoning benchmarks

D6.constraint_headline: No AI-specific binding framework enacted; AISI evaluations voluntary; anticipated UK AI bill did not materialise

D7.qualification_headline: UK Compute Roadmap commits up to £2 billion for public AI compute infrastructure, confirmed by Parliament

D1.constraint_headline: No commercially marketed datacenter AI training accelerator with domestically controlled core microarchitectural IP

D2.constraint_headline: No ≤7nm wafer fab on Indian territory; most advanced approved project (Tata Dholera) targets 28nm-110nm

D3.constraint_headline: No Indian sole-source position in the ≤7nm semiconductor equipment supply chain

D4.constraint_headline: Largest sovereign compute allocation (4,096 H100s, IndiaAI Mission) empirically exercised; produced sub-frontier result

D5.constraint_headline: Sole candidate (Sarvam-105B) trails absolute May 2026 frontier; independent evaluation limited

D6.constraint_headline: No AI-specific statute in force; DPDPA core obligations delayed to May 2027; IT Rules 2026 limited to content moderation

D7.qualification_headline: IndiaAI Mission budgets Rs 10,372 crore across seven pillars with a 10,000 GPU target; compute runs on US ecosystem infrastructure

D1.constraint_headline: Habana Labs/Gaudi IP wholly owned by Intel US; no independent Israeli training-class accelerator programme remains

D2.constraint_headline: Fab 28 runs Intel 7 (10nm-class DUV, no EUV); Fab 38 frozen since mid-2024 with no restart date

D3.qualification_headline: Nova holds sole-source metrology and Camtek is tool of reference for HBM4; limited to inspection, US-parented entities excluded

D4.constraint_headline: Largest Israeli-controlled GPU clusters are an order of magnitude below frontier training scale

D5.constraint_headline: AI21 Jamba scores 40-50 percentage points behind frontier benchmarks; company has pivoted away from scale competition

D6.constraint_headline: No enacted AI-specific law with extraterritorial reach; existing controls are privacy-based or dual-use export

D7.constraint_headline: ~$140M deployed AI-specific capital over six years; two orders of magnitude below $1B-$10B+ peer programmes

D1.constraint_headline: No domestically designed training-class accelerator in production; ARM/MIPS licenses revoked, foundry access severed

D2.constraint_headline: Most advanced operational node is 90nm at Mikron; state roadmap targets 28nm no earlier than 2028-2030

D3.constraint_headline: Best domestic lithography tool operates at 350nm on 200mm wafers; no global supply role

D4.constraint_headline: Total national AI compute 0.073 exaflops; largest cluster ~1,600 A100 nodes

D5.constraint_headline: Flagship LLMs trail frontier by 34-55% on key benchmarks; two of three built on Chinese Qwen base weights

D6.constraint_headline: No enacted AI-specific extraterritorial framework; enforcement model produces market exit, not foreign compliance

D7.constraint_headline: Executed federal AI spending ~$80-90M/year; announced budgets systematically revised downward 5-20×

D1.constraint_headline: Tenstorrent redomiciled to Delaware 2023; no Canadian-controlled accelerator in volume production

D2.constraint_headline: Zero front-end logic fabs at ≤7nm on Canadian territory; ecosystem is packaging and specialty nodes

D3.constraint_headline: US-parented entity (Teledyne Technologies); pellicle manufacturing credits to parent jurisdiction

D4.constraint_headline: No Canadian-HQ entity operates GPU clusters at frontier-training scale; all hyperscalers US-controlled

D5.constraint_headline: Cohere ranks decisively second-tier on flagship cross-domain benchmarks (GPQA, Arena, SWE-bench)

D6.constraint_headline: AIDA died on the order paper January 2025; ECL export controls bind Canadian exporters only, no extraterritorial reach

D7.constraint_headline: ~CAD $2.9B over five years (~0.02% GDP/yr); flagship SCIP supercomputer not yet operational

D1.constraint_headline: Domestic chip design is interface IP and custom ASICs, not merchant training-class AI accelerators

D2.constraint_headline: Domestic fabrication is 200 mm at 90 nm; Malaysia's role is back-end assembly, not leading-edge production

D3.constraint_headline: Equipment makers in Malaysia are foreign-headquartered (Lam, ASML); no domestically-controlled advanced-equipment capability exists

D4.constraint_headline: Operational AI compute is a 20-megawatt facility; no frontier-scale training runs at the assessment date

D5.constraint_headline: Domestic models lead only the self-built MalayMMLU benchmark; none ranks top-tier on broadly-recognised leaderboards

D6.constraint_headline: A territorial chip-transit control that adopts United States export-regulation scope, without independent extraterritorial enforcement reach

D7.qualification_headline: Budget 2026, enacted as Supply Act 2026, directs sovereign capital to a national AI and semiconductor programme

D1.constraint_headline: No Singapore-domiciled designer of training-class AI accelerators; domestic AI-silicon is edge-focused

D2.constraint_headline: Domestic fabrication is mature- and specialty-node; no leading-edge production, new capacity not operational until 2028

D3.constraint_headline: Front-end equipment is a foreign oligopoly; Singapore's capability is back-end assembly and consumables, not advanced tools

D4.constraint_headline: On-territory training is sub-frontier (72 H100s); the frontier-scale build is sited off-territory in Johor

D5.constraint_headline: Domestic models lead Southeast-Asian-language tasks on a foreign base; none ranks top-tier on general leaderboards

D6.constraint_headline: Voluntary soft-law AI governance; export controls bind domestically only, no extraterritorial reach

D7.qualification_headline: Budget 2024 directs sovereign capital to a national AI programme, with a ring-fenced commitment to high-performance compute

D1.constraint_headline: Domestic chip activity is inference- or design-only with foreign IP; no UAE merchant training-class accelerator

D2.constraint_headline: No operational leading-edge (sub-7nm) fabrication; the UAE is import-dependent, prospective foreign fabs at feasibility stage

D3.constraint_headline: No UAE-headquartered advanced-equipment maker; the UAE procures foreign chips as a capital provider, EUV supplied solely by ASML

D4.constraint_headline: On-territory compute is sub-frontier; the flagship cluster is pre-operational and foreign-operated, frontier-scale G42 compute US-sited

D5.constraint_headline: Domestically directed models, not top-tier; K2 Think V2 leads competition maths but trails on knowledge benchmarks

D6.constraint_headline: No AI-specific extraterritorial authority; the extraterritorial instruments are child-safety and data-protection regimes, with no cross-border AI enforcement

D7.qualification_headline: Abu Dhabi Law No. 3 of 2024 and the state vehicle MGX direct sovereign capital into a nationally-transformative AI programme

D1.constraint_headline: No Saudi accelerator designer; the sole Riyadh design centre is Qualcomm's, and the sovereign chip programme was abandoned in 2026

D2.constraint_headline: No fabrication at any node on Saudi territory; the Alat chip programme was abandoned in 2026

D3.constraint_headline: No Saudi-headquartered advanced-equipment maker; EUV is ASML-only and the major equipment is US, Dutch or Japanese

D4.constraint_headline: On-territory frontier-scale compute is under construction, not live; the operational clusters are academic HPC or inference-only

D5.constraint_headline: ALLaM is Saudi-controlled but Arabic-specialist — it tops an Arabic-only leaderboard, not top-tier across three general domains

D6.constraint_headline: No AI framework with extraterritorial reach; AI governance is non-binding and territorial, the draft Global AI Hub Law unenacted

D7.qualification_headline: Through HUMAIN, the Public Investment Fund directs sovereign capital into AI compute and infrastructure at nationally transformative scale

D1.constraint_headline: STMicroelectronics is foreign-incorporated and below leading-edge; indigenous ventures build design-automation tools, not training accelerators

D2.constraint_headline: No sub-7 nm fabrication on Swiss territory; the most advanced Swiss-linked fabrication is located in France and Italy

D3.constraint_headline: Swiss advanced-equipment makers (VAT, Comet) are market-leading but substitutable, not sole sources of any sub-7 nm-essential component

D4.constraint_headline: The one frontier-scale system (Alps) is research-only; the commercially available platforms are well below frontier scale

D5.constraint_headline: A sovereign, fully-open model (Apertus-70B) with Swiss-controlled IP, but sub-frontier in capability and not top-tier on broad leaderboards

D6.constraint_headline: AI governance is sector-specific and applies primarily to state actors; extraterritorial reach is confined to general data protection, not AI

D7.constraint_headline: A discrete CHF 20 million grant (CHF 5 million in 2026), not a structural reorientation; no national AI programme

D1.constraint_headline: Domestic semiconductor activity is chip design and packaging, not training-accelerator design; no domestically-controlled accelerator designer

D2.constraint_headline: No sub-7 nm fabrication on Brazilian territory; the only foundry (CEITEC) tops out at 350 nm

D3.constraint_headline: No domestic advanced-equipment maker; the category is foreign-held (ASML, US, Japan), Brazil's footprint is design and packaging

D4.constraint_headline: Santos Dumont is a sub-frontier research HPC; the transformative buildout (Piauí) is foreign-operated and unbuilt

D5.constraint_headline: Sovereign, territorially-controlled model IP (Sabiá, SoberanIA), but sub-frontier and specialised to Brazilian-Portuguese domains

D6.constraint_headline: PL 2338 is unenacted and domestic-territorial, with no extraterritorial-scope provision; no demonstrated reach beyond Brazil

D7.constraint_headline: A genuine R$23 billion plan, but mostly credit and private/foreign-operated capital; the sovereign AI compute is research-grade, not transformative

dimension_watch.trigger: Mistral Compute sustained frontier-class training runs
Mistral release closing approximately 35% frontier gap
Independent verification of Sarvam-105B benchmark claims
FY2026 budget blocked by opposition in Legislative Yuan
STACKIT and sovereign AI programme scaling
Nagel Committee NIS 25 billion legislatively appropriated
Cohere–Aleph Alpha merger closes and combined capability assessed
SCIP operational and Budget 2026 scales commitment
